_metadata.title: ComplianceLint Interim Standard for EU AI Act Article 9: Risk Management System
_metadata.disclaimer: This is an interim standard developed by ComplianceLint based on available guidance, industry best practices, and existing risk management standards. It is NOT an official standard. It will be superseded when CEN-CENELEC publishes the harmonized standard for Article 9 (expected Q4 2026).
basis: EU AI Act Recitals 65-67 (legislative intent on risk management)
ISO 14971:2019 — Medical device risk management (adapted for AI)
NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)
_metadata.awaiting_standard: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for Art. 9 (expected Q4 2026)

requirements.title: Risk Management System Documentation
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST establish, implement, document, and maintain a risk management system throughout the entire lifecycle of the high-risk AI system.
requirements.rationale: Art. 9(1) mandates a continuous, documented risk management process — not a one-time assessment. The system must be a living document updated throughout the AI lifecycle.
acceptable_implementations: Dedicated risk_assessment.md or risk management plan document
Risk register (spreadsheet, database, or structured file) with identified risks
Formal FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) document
ISO 14971 or ISO/IEC 23894 compliant risk management file
Risk management section in system design documentation
not_acceptable: One-time risk assessment with no update mechanism
Risk management only in meeting notes or emails

requirements.title: Risk Identification and Analysis
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST identify and analyze known and reasonably foreseeable risks that the high-risk AI system can pose to health, safety, or fundamental rights.
requirements.rationale: Art. 9(2)(a) requires systematic identification of risks from intended use AND reasonably foreseeable misuse. This must go beyond technical failures to include societal impacts.
required_content: List of identified risks with severity and likelihood ratings
Analysis of risks from intended use scenarios
Analysis of risks from reasonably foreseeable misuse
Consideration of risks to health, safety, and fundamental rights
acceptable_implementations: Risk register with risk ID, description, category, likelihood, severity, and risk score
FMEA table with failure modes, effects, severity, occurrence, and detection ratings
Structured threat modeling document (STRIDE, LINDDUN for privacy)
NIST AI RMF MAP function outputs

requirements.title: Post-Market Risk Evaluation
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST evaluate risks using data gathered from post-market monitoring systems (Art. 72).
requirements.rationale: Art. 9(2)(c) requires ongoing risk evaluation based on real-world usage data. Pre-deployment risk assessment alone is insufficient.
acceptable_implementations: Documented process for incorporating post-market monitoring findings into risk register
Incident tracking linked to risk assessment updates
Periodic risk review schedule tied to monitoring data
Feedback loop from deployment metrics to risk scores

requirements.title: Risk Management Measures
requirements.requirement: Risk management measures MUST eliminate or reduce risks as far as possible through adequate design and development. Remaining residual risks MUST be mitigated and controlled. Measures MUST consider cumulative effects and be proportionate.
requirements.rationale: Art. 9(3) establishes a hierarchy: eliminate > reduce > mitigate > communicate. Measures must be proportionate to the level of risk and consider effects in combination, not in isolation.
required_content: For each identified risk: mitigation strategy with rationale
Evidence that elimination was considered before mitigation
Assessment of cumulative/combined effects of multiple risks
acceptable_implementations: Risk treatment plan with risk ID linked to specific mitigations
Design controls document showing risk-driven design decisions
Code-level safety checks, guardrails, and input validation linked to risk items
Operational controls and monitoring procedures for residual risks

requirements.title: Testing with Appropriate Metrics and Thresholds
requirements.requirement: The high-risk AI system MUST be tested to identify the most appropriate and targeted risk management measures. Testing MUST use appropriate metrics and probabilistic thresholds.
requirements.rationale: Art. 9(4) requires that risk management is validated through testing — not just documented. Tests must use quantitative metrics, not just qualitative assessments.
required_content: Test plans linked to identified risks
Quantitative metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1, AUC, etc.)
Defined probabilistic thresholds for acceptable performance
Test results documenting whether thresholds were met
Evaluation against intended purpose and foreseeable conditions
acceptable_implementations: Automated test suites with documented coverage of risk scenarios
Benchmark evaluation scripts with threshold assertions
Model evaluation reports with metrics and pass/fail criteria
Red-teaming or adversarial testing results
Performance evaluation across demographic subgroups (fairness testing)
not_acceptable: Tests without quantitative metrics or thresholds
Manual-only testing with no reproducible results

requirements.title: Child-Specific Risk Assessment
requirements.requirement: If the high-risk AI system is intended to be used by or affect children, the risk management system MUST specifically assess child-specific risks.
requirements.rationale: Art. 9(5) explicitly requires that systems intended for children account for their specific vulnerabilities. This is a distinct obligation beyond general risk assessment.
acceptable_implementations: Dedicated section in risk assessment for child-specific risks
Age-appropriate design assessment
Child safety impact analysis
Consultation with child development or protection experts
requirements.note: This requirement only applies when the system is intended to interact with or be used by children. If not applicable, document the non-applicability.

requirements.title: Residual Risk Communication
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST communicate residual risks to deployers via instructions for use (Art. 13) and, where appropriate, to affected persons.
requirements.rationale: Art. 9(6-8) requires that risks which cannot be eliminated or sufficiently mitigated are transparently communicated to downstream users and affected persons.
acceptable_implementations: Residual risk section in user documentation or instructions for use
Known limitations document listing unmitigated risks
Model card with risk and limitation disclosures
Safety datasheet or risk communication document

scoring.compliant: R01 met + R02 met + R04 or R05 met + at least 3 others met
scoring.partial: R01 met + at least 2 of R02-R07 met
scoring.non_compliant: R01 not met, or fewer than 2 of R02-R07 met
